mnemonics: JToggleButton is like a light switch - it's either ON (selected) or OFF (deselected), unlike a regular button which just gets pressed and released.
keyPoints: Extends AbstractButton, inheriting many common button properties
Can be used standalone or in a ButtonGroup for mutually exclusive selection
Fires ItemEvents and ActionEvents when its state changes
commonMistakes: Forgetting to add ToggleButtons to a ButtonGroup when wanting radio-button behavior (mutual exclusion)
Using ActionListener instead of ItemListener to detect selection state changes
Not setting initial selection state, leaving all buttons deselected by default
